findings.General: Mild distress, sitting upright, using accessory muscles

findings.HEENT: Mild perioral cyanosis; pupils equal, round, and reactive to light; no lymphadenopathy

findings.Cardiovascular: S1 and S2 present, regular rate and rhythm; no murmurs, rubs, or gallops

findings.Pulmonary: Diffuse expiratory wheezing bilaterally; prolonged expiratory phase; decreased air entry at bilateral bases

findings.Abdomen: Soft, nontender, nondistended; positive bowel sounds

findings.Extremities: Trace pitting edema to bilateral ankles; no clubbing or cyanosis

findings.Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3; no focal deficits

diagnosisOptions: Urgent surgical coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG)

findings.HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic; pupils equal and reactive; no JVD appreciated at 45 degrees

findings.Cardiovascular: Tachycardic; S1 and S2 present; S4 gallop noted; no murmurs or rubs

findings.Abdomen: Soft, nontender, nondistended; positive bowel sounds

findings.Extremities: Cool and clammy; no peripheral edema; pulses 2+ throughout

findings.Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3; anxious but no focal neurologic deficits

findings.General: Fatigued-appearing woman in no acute distress; affect flat and sluggish

findings.HEENT: Coarse, dry hair with diffuse thinning; periorbital puffiness; thyroid mildly enlarged and non-tender; no lymphadenopathy

findings.Cardiovascular: Bradycardic; S1 and S2 present; no murmurs, rubs, or gallops

findings.Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally; no wheezing or crackles

findings.Abdomen: Soft, mildly distended; hypoactive bowel sounds; no tenderness

findings.Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3; psychomotor slowing; no focal deficits

findings.General: Distressed male, lying still in a darkened room; vomited once in triage

findings.HEENT: Photophobia present; pupils equal and sluggishly reactive bilaterally; no papilledema on fundoscopy; nuchal rigidity present

findings.Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm; S1 and S2 present; no murmurs

findings.Abdomen: Soft, nontender, nondistended; normoactive bowel sounds

findings.Neurologic: GCS 14 (E4V4M6); Kernig's sign positive; Brudzinski's sign positive; no focal motor deficits

diagnosisOptions: IV fluids -> insulin infusion -> potassium repletion monitoring
Insulin bolus immediately, then IV fluids
Sodium bicarbonate infusion to correct acidosis first
Oral rehydration and subcutaneous insulin correction dose

findings.General: Ill-appearing, lethargic young male; fruity odor on breath

findings.HEENT: Dry mucous membranes; sunken eyes; pupils equal and reactive; no lymphadenopathy

findings.Cardiovascular: Tachycardic; S1 and S2 present; no murmurs; capillary refill 3 seconds

findings.Pulmonary: Kussmaul respirations (deep, labored, rapid); clear to auscultation

findings.Abdomen: Diffuse mild tenderness to palpation; no guarding or rebound; hypoactive bowel sounds

findings.Extremities: Skin turgor decreased; cool peripheries; no edema
